Title: Category Navigation - Main Menu
Preconditions: User logged in; catalog populated
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Hover over main menu navigation
[WAIT] Wait for category dropdown to appear
[ASSERT] Verify dropdown is visible
[ACT] Click on a top-level category
[WAIT] Wait for category page to load
[ASSERT] Verify category page content
Assertions:
[DOM] Main menu displays top-level categories
[DOM] Subcategory items visible on hover
[NAV] Category page loads with correct URL slug
[DOM] Category heading matches selected category
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET category page returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Spinner >5s; dropdown not appearing; category page returns 4xx; console TypeError

[DOM] Add to cart button present on each card

[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on category page request

Title: Category - Facet Filtering
Preconditions: Category with faceted products exists

[ACT] Click on a facet value (e.g., Brand)
[WAIT] Wait for product list to update
[ASSERT] Verify products are filtered; result count updates
[ACT] Apply an additional facet value from another group
[WAIT] Wait for product list to update
[ASSERT] Verify combined AND filtering — result set narrower
Assertions:
[DOM] Facet panel visible with multiple facet groups
[DOM] Clicking a facet value filters the product list
[MATH] Multiple facets combine with AND logic — result count <= each individual filter count
[DOM] Result count label updates after each filter action
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on filter request
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Spinner >5s after filter click; facet panel not rendering; result count unchanged after filter; console TypeError

Preconditions: Products with numeric characters in their name or SKU exist in catalog; at least one product has a numeric SKU segment
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Click the search input
[ACT] fill 'Search': a 4-6 digit numeric string that is a substring of a known product SKU (e.g., '6460')
[WAIT] Wait for autocomplete dropdown to appear
[ASSERT] Verify the autocomplete dropdown shows products or hints related to the numeric query
[KEY] press [Enter]
[WAIT] Wait for search results page to load
[ASSERT] Verify the results page loads without error (not a blank page or 500 error)
[ASSERT] Verify result count >= 0 with meaningful state (results found or no-results empty state)
Assertions:
[DOM] Autocomplete shows product suggestions or hints for numeric partial query
[STATE] Search results page loads without JS errors for a pure numeric query
[DOM] Results or empty state shown with no error page or 5xx for numeric query
[STATE] Numeric-only query handled gracefully by the search engine
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (searchProducts) returns HTTP 200 for numeric query
[NETWORK] no 5xx server errors for numeric-only search
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Numeric query causing 500 error; autocomplete crashing on numeric input; search results page blank for numeric query; console TypeError on numeric input

Title: Search Results Next and Previous Page Navigation Controls Work Correctly
Preconditions: Search returns 2+ pages of results; pagination enabled
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and perform a broad search returning 2+ pages
[KEY] press [Enter]
[WAIT] Wait for search results page
[ASSERT] Verify result count exceeds 1 page worth of results
[ASSERT] Verify pagination controls rendered: 'Previous' and page numbers and 'Next'
[ASSERT] Verify 'Previous' is disabled or absent on page 1
[ACT] Click 'Next' or page number 2
[WAIT] Wait for page 2 to load
[ASSERT] Verify 'Previous' is now enabled on page 2
[ASSERT] Verify page 2 indicator is active in the pagination control
[ACT] Click 'Previous' to return to page 1
[WAIT] Wait for page 1 to reload
[ASSERT] Verify page 1 indicator is active and 'Previous' is again disabled or absent
Assertions:
[DOM] Pagination controls with Previous and page numbers and Next visible for 2+ page result set
[STATE] 'Previous' disabled or absent on first page; enabled on page 2
[STATE] Active page indicator updates correctly when navigating pages
[STATE] Correct page content shown after each navigation action
[DOM] 'Next' button visible and enabled when more pages exist
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (searchProducts) fires with updated offset or page on each navigation
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on pagination requests
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: 'Previous' active on first page; 'Next' absent even when results span multiple pages; page indicator not updating; same products on both pages; console TypeError

Title: Sort Persists Across Search Pagination Pages
Preconditions: Search returns 2+ pages of results; sort options available on search results page
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} and perform a search returning 2+ pages
[KEY] press [Enter]
[WAIT] Wait for search results page
[ASSERT] Verify sort dropdown is present
[ACT] Select 'Price: Low to High' from the sort dropdown
[WAIT] Wait for products to reorder
[ASSERT] Verify first product has lowest price in the visible set
[ASSERT] Note the sort dropdown value shows 'Price: Low to High'
[ACT] Click 'Next' or page 2 in pagination
[WAIT] Wait for page 2 to load
[ASSERT] Verify sort dropdown on page 2 still shows 'Price: Low to High'
[ASSERT] Verify all products on page 2 have prices greater than or equal to the last product on page 1
[ASSERT] Verify the URL contains both the sort parameter and the page parameter
Assertions:
[STATE] Sort selection 'Price: Low to High' persists when navigating to page 2
[MATH] Prices on page 2 are >= the last price on page 1 confirming ascending order maintained across pages
[DOM] Sort dropdown on page 2 reflects the same sort as page 1
[NAV] URL on page 2 includes both sort and page parameters
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (searchProducts) on page 2 includes both sort and page/offset params
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Sort dropdown resetting to default when moving to page 2; prices on page 2 not continuing the ascending order; URL losing sort param on pagination; console TypeError

Title: Breadcrumb Resets When Navigating from Subcategory Back to Parent via Main Menu
Preconditions: Category hierarchy exists with at least 2 levels; user can navigate subcategory then return to parent via main menu
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Click on a top-level category in the main navigation menu
[WAIT] Wait for top-level category page to load
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Top-Level Category]
[ACT] Click on a subcategory link within the top-level category page
[WAIT] Wait for subcategory page to load
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Top-Level Category] > [Subcategory]
[ACT] Click on the top-level category link in the main navigation menu (not the breadcrumb)
[WAIT] Wait for top-level category page to load
[ASSERT] Verify URL updated to top-level category URL (subcategory segment absent)
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Top-Level Category] (subcategory name must be absent)
[ASSERT] Verify category heading matches top-level category name
Assertions:
[DOM] breadcrumb trail contains exactly 2 items: Home + Top-Level Category
[DOM] subcategory name absent from breadcrumb after navigating to parent
[NAV] URL matches top-level category URL (no subcategory slug)
[DOM] category page heading matches top-level category name
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no Vue hydration warnings or TypeError
[NETWORK] GET top-level category page returns HTTP 200
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on navigation
Failure_Signals: breadcrumb still shows subcategory name after navigating to parent; URL correct but breadcrumb stale; heading correct but breadcrumb wrong (VCST-4728 regression check)

[ASSERT] Verify category heading matches top-level category

Title: Breadcrumb Reflects Correct Path When Navigating Between Sibling Subcategories
Preconditions: Category hierarchy with one parent and at least 2 sibling subcategories
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a subcategory page (e.g., Home > [Parent] > [Subcategory A])

[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Parent] > [Subcategory A]
[ACT] Click on the parent category in the main navigation menu
[WAIT] Wait for parent category page to load
[ACT] Click on a different sibling subcategory (e.g., [Subcategory B])
[WAIT] Wait for sibling subcategory page to load
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Parent] > [Subcategory B]
[ASSERT] Verify [Subcategory A] is absent from the breadcrumb
Assertions:
[DOM] breadcrumb shows Home > [Parent] > [New Sibling] — no trace of previous sibling
[DOM] breadcrumb has exactly 3 items matching the new navigation path
[NAV] URL reflects the new sibling subcategory slug
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or hydration warnings
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx
Failure_Signals: breadcrumb shows blend of both paths; previous subcategory name persists; breadcrumb shows 4 items instead of 3; heading correct but breadcrumb wrong

Title: Breadcrumb Determinism on Rapid Forward-Back-Forward Navigation
Preconditions: Category hierarchy with at least 2 levels; user can navigate quickly
Steps:

[WAIT] Wait for page to load

[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Top-Level Category] > [Subcategory] (final destination)
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb does not show intermediate stale state
[ASSERT] Verify URL matches subcategory
Assertions:
[DOM] breadcrumb shows 3 items: Home > [Top-Level Category] > [Subcategory] matching final destination
[DOM] no intermediate stale breadcrumb state visible
[NAV] URL is subcategory URL
[DOM] heading matches subcategory name
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no race condition errors or uncaught exceptions
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx
Failure_Signals: breadcrumb flickers between states; breadcrumb shows parent instead of subcategory after rapid navigation; heading correct but breadcrumb stale
